title: .evolve/ subtree paths (runs/cycle-N, worktrees, inbox, instincts) hand-built by raw filepath.Join in ~10 production sites — internal/paths models only top-level files, so the layout cannot evolve in one place
summary: paths.Layout is the documented layout SSOT but only for top-level files (StateFile, LedgerFile, ...). The runs/cycle-N form alone is derived in ≥6 sites: core/runworkspace.go:49, runscope/runscope.go:133 ('Mirrors core.RunWorkspacePath' comment admits it), budgethistory/budgethistory.go:58, acssuite/acssuite.go:560, cmd_loop_control.go:183; plus worktrees/inbox/instincts variants (core/worktree.go:51, cmd_loop_outcome.go:226, bridge/driver_tmux_repl.go:455, bridge/interaction_rules.go:48, swarm/provision.go:101, pruneephemeral.go:61,65). The runscope comment carries a load-bearing invariant (no lane embedding) that other sites cannot see. Same wound class that forced core.ResolveCycleStatePath SSOT after a fleet isolation hole.
root_cause: Layout knowledge duplicated per consumer because paths never grew subtree accessors; invariants live in comments at one of many sites.
fix: Extend paths.Layout with pure projection methods: RunsDir(), RunWorkspace(cycle int), WorktreesDir(), InboxDir(), InstinctsDir(name). core.RunWorkspacePath and runscope.WorkspacePath become one-line delegators (signatures preserved — zero caller churn pass 1); remaining literal sites switch to the accessors in the same cycle (grep-verified zero remaining '.evolve','runs' joins outside paths). A future per-lane layout change then happens in ONE method.
acceptance: RED: TestLayout_RunWorkspaceProjection — accessor equals today's literal form byte-for-byte (compat pin), and delegators route through it
grep gate: no filepath.Join(..., ".evolve", "runs"...) outside internal/paths (add to the item's audit script)
go vet, -race, apicover -enforce green; behavior-neutral (paths byte-identical)
